data.title: As a developer, all hot ticket CLI ops dispatch through the sourceable library with no logic duplication
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Expand `ticket-lib-api.sh` to cover all remaining hot ops and wire the dispatcher to delegate every covered op through the library. Split scope explicitly by op class: pure-bash ops (`ticket_create`, `ticket_tag`, `ticket_untag`, `ticket_comment`, `ticket_edit`) hit the <0.05s SC1 target; thin-wrapper ops (`ticket_link` — must call `ticket-graph.py` for cycle detection; `ticket_list` — calls `ticket_reducer` for state materialization; `ticket_transition` — calls reducer for current-state read then bash-native commit) hit <0.15s because one `python3` call is intrinsic and cannot be removed without reimplementing graph/reducer logic in bash (out of scope). Write a formal extension-pattern specification at `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-lib-api-extension-pattern.md` so downstream epics (2c28-e5b9, 4931-f639, e1b2-2653) can add new ops consistently.

**Why**: Story B proved the pattern with one read op. Story D completes the library surface so the dispatcher becomes a thin wrapper for every covered subcommand — the single-source-of-truth goal (SC3). The op-class split is necessary because `ticket-graph.py` cycle detection and `ticket_reducer` state materialization are legitimately Python — bash-rehoming them is out of scope for this epic. Publishing the extension pattern protects the design from drift as downstream epics add new subcommands.

**Scope**:
- IN: Library functions `ticket_create`, `ticket_tag`, `ticket_untag`, `ticket_comment`, `ticket_edit` (pure-bash; <0.05s); library functions `ticket_link`, `ticket_list`, `ticket_transition` (thin-wrapper with at most one python3 call per invocation; <0.15s); dispatcher routes all 9 covered subcommands through `_ticketlib_dispatch`; `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` honored per op (each rehomed function falls back to its pre-refactor path); `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-lib-api-extension-pattern.md` covering function naming (`_ticketlib_<op>`), argv parsing convention, error return codes, write/read integration points, sourceability checklist.
- OUT: Non-hot ops (`ticket archive`, `ticket unarchive`, `ticket deps`, `ticket sync`, `ticket compact`, `ticket fsck`, `ticket bridge-*`) continue to exec their subcommand scripts unchanged; platform matrix testing (Story F); sourceability regression suite (Story E).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `ticket-lib-api.sh` exposes `ticket_create`, `ticket_tag`, `ticket_untag`, `ticket_comment`, `ticket_edit`, `ticket_link`, `ticket_list`, `ticket_transition` — in addition to `ticket_show` from Story B
  ← Satisfies: "The CLI dispatcher sources the library and delegates all covered operations to library functions — no logic duplication"
- When this story is complete, the CLI dispatcher routes every covered subcommand through `_ticketlib_dispatch` — no exec'd `.sh` scripts for the 9 covered ops; uncovered ops unchanged
  ← Satisfies: "The CLI dispatcher sources the library and delegates all covered operations to library functions — no logic duplication"
- When this story is complete, pure-bash ops (`ticket_create`, `ticket_tag`, `ticket_untag`, `ticket_comment`, `ticket_edit`) complete in <0.05s per call on Linux reference hardware via hyperfine over 10 warm runs
  ← Satisfies: "Write-path operations ... complete in <0.05s per call"
- When this story is complete, thin-wrapper ops (`ticket_link`, `ticket_list`, `ticket_transition`) complete in <0.15s per call on Linux reference hardware via hyperfine over 10 warm runs
  ← Satisfies: "Read-path operations (ticket show, list) complete in <0.15s per call"
- When this story is complete, each rehomed op honors `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` by routing through its pre-refactor codepath
  ← Satisfies: "A `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` env flag reverts to the pre-refactor Python-backed path, available for at least the first release cycle"
- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-lib-api-extension-pattern.md` is published and describes the naming convention, argv parsing, error return codes, write/read integration points, and sourceability checklist for adding new ops
  ← Satisfies: "The CLI dispatcher sources the library and delegates all covered operations to library functions"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Single source of truth — dispatcher path and library path share one implementation; any op logic change must happen in exactly one place
- [Performance] The op-class split makes SC1/SC2 targets meaningful per op; SC6's >10% regression gate compares per-op metrics against A0 baselines

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
